title: Comparing center and spread
description: Practice comparing the shape, center, and spread between two distributions of a quantitative variable.

assessment_items.question_content:
Minneapolis, Minnesota and Portland, Oregon have very similar latitudes. Latitude measures how far north or south a place is, which in turn affects the temperature.

The histograms below show the daily high temperatures (in degrees Celsius) that were recorded during January $2013$ in each city.



[[☃ image 1]]

  


[[☃ image 2]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the Minneapolis distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the Portland distribution.

The variability in the Minneapolis distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the Portland distribution.
hints: We can't calculate the mean values of each distribution, but we can see visually that the center of the Minneapolis distribution is lower than the center of the Portland distribution.

We can see visually that the Minneapolis distribution is more spread out than the Portland distribution. We can't calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see that the range of the Minneapolis distribution is much larger.

In conclusion:

The center of the Minneapolis distribution is less than the center of the Portland distribution.

The variability in the Minneapolis distribution is greater than the variability in the Portland distribution.

options.alt: A histogram labeled Portland. The vertical axis is labeled Number of days. The horizontal axis is labeled Temperature in degrees Celsius. The data is shown as a cluster of bars ranging from 1 through 13. The peak is the bar from 4 to 7 and is 12 high.

A histogram labeled Minneapolis. The vertical axis is labeled Number of days. The horizontal axis is labeled Temperature in degrees Celsius. The data is shown as a cluster of bars ranging from negative 20 through 7. The highest bar is from 1 to 4 and is 7 high.

assessment_items.question_content:
Minneapolis, Minnesota and Portland, Oregon have very similar latitudes. Latitude measures how far north or south a place is, which in turn affects the temperature.

The histograms below show the daily high temperatures (in degrees Celsius) that were recorded during July $2013$ in each city.


[[☃ image 1]]

  


[[☃ image 2]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the Minneapolis distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the Portland distribution.

The variability in the Minneapolis distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the Portland distribution.
hints: We can't calculate the mean values of each distribution, but we can see visually that the center of the Minneapolis distribution is greater than the center of the Portland distribution.

We can see visually that the Minneapolis distribution is more spread out than the Portland distribution. We can't calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see that the range of the Minneapolis distribution is larger.

In conclusion:

The variability in the Minneapolis distribution is greater than the variability in the Portland distribution.

options.alt: A histogram labeled Portland. The vertical axis is labeled Number of days. The horizontal axis is labeled Temperature in degrees Celsius. The data is shown as a cluster of bars ranging from 21 through 36. The peak is the bar from 24 through 27.

A histogram labeled Minneapolis. The vertical axis is labeled Number of days. The horizontal axis is labeled Temperature in degrees Celsius. The data is shown as a cluster of bars ranging from 21 through 36. The two highest bars are from 30 to 36. There is a bar from 15 to 18 that is up one.

assessment_items.question_content:
People seem to like movies about comic book heroes. 

The histograms below show the gross revenue (in hundreds of millions of dollars) of movies based on heroes from the two most popular comic book companies.  



[[☃ image 1]]

  


[[☃ image 2]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the Marble comics distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the DX comics distribution.

The variability in the Marble comics distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the DX comics distribution.
hints: We can't calculate the mean values of each distribution, but we can see visually that the center of the Marble comics distribution is greater than the center of the DX comics distribution.

We can see visually that the Marble comics distribution is more spread out than the DX comics distribution. We can't calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see that the range of the Marble distribution is larger.

In conclusion:

The center of the Marble comics distribution is greater than the center of the DX comics distribution.

The variability in the Marble comics distribution is greater than the variability in the DX comics distribution.

options.alt: A histogram labeled DX comics. The vertical axis is labeled Number of movies. The horizontal axis is labeled Revenue in hundreds of millions of dollars. Most of the data is clustered between the bins ranging from 0 to 3 with the highest bar from 0 to 1 with a height of 11.

A histogram labeled Marble comics. The vertical axis is labeled Number of movies. The horizontal axis is labeled Revenue in hundreds of millions of dollars. Most of the data is clustered between the bins ranging from 0 to 5 with the highest bar from 1 to 2 with a height of 15.

assessment_items.question_content:
At the Olympic games, many events have several rounds of competition. One of these events is the men's $100$-meter backstroke. 

The upper dot plot shows the times (in seconds) of the top $8$ finishers in the final round of the $2012$ Olympics. The lower dot plot shows the times of the same $8$ swimmers, but in the semifinal round.



[[☃ image 1]]

The center of the semifinal round distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the final round distribution.

The variability in the semifinal round distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the final round distribution.
hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution because we can see visually that the center of the semifinal round distribution is greater than the center of the final round distribution.
We could calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see visually that the semifinal round distribution is less spread out than the final round distribution.

The center of the semifinal round distribution is greater than the center of the final round distribution.

The variability in the semifinal round distribution is less than the variability in the final round distribution.

options.alt: A double dot plot with the upper half modeling the Final Round and the lower half models the Semifinal Round. The number line is labeled time in seconds. The Final Round dots range from 56 to 57 and 9 tenths with no clusters of dots. The Semifinal Round dots range from 56 and 7 tenths to 57 and 8 tenths.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Baltimore Ravens won the American football championship in $2013$. In the same season, the Kansas City Chiefs had a record of $2$ wins and $14$ losses. 

The dot plots below show the $40$-yard dash times (in seconds) of the defensive backs on each team's roster. Defensive backs mainly defend against passes, and the $40$-yard dash is the main measure of speed used in American football.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/148e1f66ec6f2e566a5b5e53a2f7c70cf828e1f2)

The center of the Ravens distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the Chiefs distribution.

The variability in the Ravens distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the Chiefs distribution.
hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution because we can see visually that the center of the Ravens distribution is less than the center of the Chiefs distribution.
We could calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see visually that the Ravens distribution is more spread out than the Chiefs distribution.

The center of the Ravens distribution is less than the center of the Chiefs distribution.

The variability in the Ravens distribution is greater than the variability in the Chiefs distribution.

assessment_items.question_content:
Before sending track and field athletes to the Olympics, the U.S. holds a qualifying meet. 

The lower dot plot shows the times (in seconds) of the $8$ sprinters who competed in the final of the women's $100$-meter dash at the $2012$ Olympic games. The upper dot plot shows the times (in seconds) of the top $8$ sprinters at the U.S. qualifying meet for that event.



[[☃ image 1]]

The center of the Olympic final distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the US qualifier distribution.

The variability in the Olympic final distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the US qualifier distribution.
hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution because we can see visually that the center of the Olympic final distribution is less than the center of the US qualifier distribution.
We could calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see visually that the Olympic final distribution is more spread out than the US qualifier distribution.

The center of the Olympic final distribution is less than the center of the US qualifier distribution.

The variability in the Olympic final distribution is greater than the variability in the US qualifier distribution.

options.alt: A double dot plot with the upper half modeling the U S qualifier and the lower half models the Olympic final. The number line is labeled time in seconds. The U S Qualifier dots range from 10 and 94 hundredths to 11 and 18 hundredths. There is a cluster of the dots ranging from 11 and 8 hundredths to 11 and 17 hundredths. The Olympic final dots have no cluster and range from 10 and 75 hundredths to 11 and 1 hundredth.

assessment_items.question_content:
Before sending track and field athletes to the Olympics, the U.S. holds a qualifying meet. 

The upper dot plot shows the distances (in meters) of the top $8$ shot putters in the preliminary round of the $2012$ Olympic games. The lower dot plot shows the top $8$ throwers in the shot put at the U.S. qualifying meet.



[[☃ image 1]]

The center of the Olympic preliminary distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the US qualifier distribution.

The variability in the Olympic preliminary distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the US qualifier distribution.
hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution because we can see visually that the center of the Olympic preliminary distribution is greater than the center of the US qualifier distribution.
We could calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see visually that the Olympic preliminary distribution is less spread out than the US qualifier distribution.

The center of the Olympic preliminary distribution is greater than the center of the US qualifier distribution.

The variability in the Olympic preliminary distribution is less than the variability in the US qualifier distribution.

options.alt: A double dot plot with the upper half modeling the Olympic preliminary and the lower half models the U S qualifier. The number line is labeled distance in meters. The Olympic preliminary dots range from 20 and 4 tenths to 21 and 4 tenths. The U S qualifier dots range from 19 and 1 tenth to 21 and 2 tenths.

assessment_items.question_content:
Macaws are tropical birds that can live a very long time. The box plots below show the lifespans (in years) of a random sample of macaws that lived in captivity and a random sample that lived in the wild.



[[☃ image 1]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the captivity distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the wild distribution.

The variability in the captivity distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the wild distribution.
hints: We can see that the median of the captivity distribution is greater than the median of the wild distribution.
We could calculate the range and IQR, but we can see visually that both the range and the IQR of the captivity distribution are much less than those of the wild distribution.

In conclusion:

The center of the captivity distribution is greater than the center of the wild distribution.

The variability in the captivity distribution is less than the variability in the wild distribution.

options.alt: A double box and whisker plot where the number line is labeled Lifespan in years. One box and whisker plot is labeled Captivity and ranges from 65 to around 84. Its box ranges from 73 to 80. The vertical line that divides the box is around 76. The other box and whisker plot is labeled Wild. It ranges from 32 to 77. Its box ranges from 48 to 62. The vertical line that divdes the box is around 54.

assessment_items.question_content:
Before sending track and field athletes to the Olympics, the U.S. holds a qualifying meet. 

The upper box plot shows distances (in meters) achieved by the top $12$ men's long jumpers at the U.S. qualifying meet. The lower box plot shows the distances achieved in the men's long jump at the $2012$ Olympic games.



[[☃ image 1]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the US qualifier distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the Olympics distribution.

The variability in the US qualifier distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the Olympics distribution.
hints: We can see that the median of the US qualifier distribution is less than the median of the Olympics distribution.
We could calculate the range and IQR, but we can see visually that both the range and the IQR of the US qualifier distribution are less than those of the Olympics distribution.

In conclusion:

The center of the US qualifier distribution is less than the center of the Olympics distribution.

The variability in the US qualifier distribution is less than the variability in the Olympics distribution.

options.alt: A double box and whisker plot where the number line is labeled Distance in meters. One box and whisker plot is labeled U S Qualifier and ranges from around 7 and 68 hundredths to around 8. Its box ranges from 7 and 69 hundredths to 7 and 9 tenths. The vertical line that divides the box is around 7 and 75 hundredths. The other box and whisker plot is labeled Olympics. It ranges from 7 and 7 tenths to 8 and 31 hundredths. Its box ranges from 7 and 82 hundredths to 8 and 12 hundredths. The vertical line that divdes the box is around 8 and 3 hundredths.

assessment_items.question_content:
St. Louis, Missouri and Washington, D.C. have very similar latitudes. Latitude measures how far north or south a place is, which in turn affects the temperature.

The box plots below show the daily high temperatures (in degrees Celsius) that were recorded during July $2013$ in each city.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/300c23f2f993ed23a2b3f8502714a2bdb625a6d7)

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the St. Louis distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the Washington, DC distribution.

The variability in the St. Louis distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the Washington, DC distribution.
hints: We can see that the median of the St. Louis distribution is less than the median of the Washington, DC distribution.
We could calculate the range and IQR, but we can see visually that both the range and the IQR of the St. Louis distribution are greater than those of the Washington, DC distribution.

In conclusion:

The center of the St. Louis distribution is less than the center of the Washington, DC distribution.

The variability in the St. Louis distribution is greater than the variability in the Washington, DC distribution.

assessment_items.question_content:
A certain company estimates the reading level of particular books in terms of the minimum grade level of student whom they expect to be able to read and comprehend the book.

The box plots below show the reading levels estimates of the top ten novels selected by a survey of young adults in $2012$ and $2015$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the $2012$ distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the $2015$ distribution.

The variability in the $2012$ distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the $2015$ distribution.
hints: We can see that the median of the $2012$ distribution is less than the median of the $2015$ distribution.
We could calculate the range and IQR, but we can see visually that both the range and the IQR of the $2012$ distribution are less than those of the $2015$ distribution.

In conclusion:

The center of the $2012$ distribution is less than the center of the $2015$ distribution.

The variability in the $2012$ distribution is less than the variability in the $2015$ distribution.

options.alt: A double box and whisker plot where the number line is labeled Reading Level. One box and whisker plot is labeled 2012 and ranges from 4 and 8 tenths to 5 and 8 tenths. Its box ranges from 4 and 8 tenths to 5 and 6 tenths. The vertical line that divides the box is around 5 and 1 tenth. The other box and whisker plot is labeled 2015. It ranges from 4.2 to 6.4. Its box ranges from 5 to 6 and 35 hundredths. The vertical line that divdes the box is around 5 and 45 hundredths.

assessment_items.question_content:
The back to back stem plot below shows the mass (in kilograms) of the offensive linemen on the University of Mount Union and the University of Alabama football teams.



[[☃ image 1]]

  

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the Mt. Union distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the Alabama distribution.

The variability in the Mt. Union distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the Alabama distribution.
hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution, because we can see visually that the center of the Mt. Union distribution is less than the center of the Alabama distribution.

We can see visually that the Mt. Union distribution is less spread out than the Alabama distribution. 

We could calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see that the range of the Alabama distribution is larger.

In conclusion:

The center of the Mt. Union distribution is less than the center of the Alabama distribution.

The variability in the Mt. Union distribution is less than the variability in the Alabama distribution.

options.alt: A double stem and leaf plot. A key indicates to multiply the stem number by 10 and add the leaf number to obtain the kilograms. Data in the chart is as follows. For Mount Union: Stem 11; 0, 0, 5, 8, 8. Stem 12; 2, 2, 7, 7, 8, 9. Stem 13; 4, 6, 6, 6, 7, 9. For Alabama: Stem 12; 0, 1, 6, 7. Stem 13; 0, 1, 2, 2, 6, 7. Stem 14; 0, 1, 2, 3, 5. Stem 15; 2, 4, 9. Stem 16; 1.

assessment_items.question_content:
The back to back stem plot below shows the heights (in centimeters) of the players on the University of Maryland women's basketball and field hockey teams.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/177649ff9c5c75acca14078843b3ea26bf34db7c)

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the basketball team distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the field hockey team distribution.

The variability in the basketball team distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the field hockey team distribution.
hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution, because we can see visually that the center of the basketball team distribution is greater than the center of the field hockey team distribution.

We can see visually that the basketball team distribution is more spread out than the field hockey team distribution. 

We could calculate MAD or IQR from this display, but we can see that the range of the basketball team distribution is larger.

In conclusion:

The center of the basketball team distribution is greater than the center of the field hockey team distribution.

The variability in the basketball team distribution is greater than the variability in the field hockey team distribution.

[[☃ image 1]]

hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution, because we can see visually that the center of the semifinal round distribution is greater than the center of the final round distribution.

options.alt: A double stem and leaf plot. A key indicates the stem number is a whole number and the leaf numbers are the decimal numbers to obtain the number of seconds. Data in the chart is as follows. For the Semifinal round: Stem 52; 7. Stem 53; 0, 3, 3, 5, 5, 5, 7. For the Final round  Stem 52; 2, 3, 8. Stem 53; 1, 2, 3, 5, 8.

assessment_items.question_content:
Shriya noted the songs played on a certain radio station for two hours.

The back to back stem plot shows the number of weeks that each song played has been on the radio station's top $100$ songs list for the hour beginning at $7\text{am}$ and the hour beginning at $11\text{am}$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

**Fill in the blanks.**

The center of the $7\text{am}$ distribution is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the center of the $11\text{am}$ distribution.

The variability in the $7\text{am}$ distribution is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the variability in the $11\text{am}$ distribution.
hints: We don't have to calculate the mean values of each distribution, because we can see visually that the center of the $7\text{am}$ distribution is greater than the center of the $11\text{am}$ distribution.

We can see visually that the $7\text{am}$ distribution is more spread out than the $11\text{am}$ distribution. 

We could calculate MAD or IQR from these displays, but we can see that the range of the $7\text{am}$ distribution is larger.

In conclusion:

The center of the $7\text{am}$ distribution is greater than the center of the $11\text{am}$ distribution.

The variability in the $7\text{am}$ distribution is greater than the variability in the $11\text{am}$ distribution.

options.alt: A double stem and leaf plot. A key indicates to multiply the stem number by 10 and add the leaf number to obtain the number of weeks. Data in the chart is as follows. For 7 A M: Stem 0; 1, 1, 5, 9. Stem 1; 1, 2, 2, 4, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9. Stem 2; 0, 0, 3, 4. Stem 3; 0. For 11 A M  Stem 0; 0, 0, 3, 3, 4, 5, 6, 6, 7, 7, 9. Stem 1; 0, 0, 1, 5, 6. Stem 2; 0.
